name: Ukraine (presidential standard)

colors.symbolism: the field — the sky over the Ukrainian steppe
the cross fleury and the trident — wheat fields, prosperity

description: A square blue field charged with a yellow cross fleury (with trefoil ends), bearing in the centre the small coat of arms of Ukraine — a yellow trident (Tryzub) on a blue square inset.
history: Adopted on 29 November 1999 by presidential decree under President Leonid Kuchma. The crosier-style cross is drawn from medieval Ruthenian and Cossack regalia; the central Tryzub is the historic emblem of the Princes of Kyiv reinstated as Ukraine's state symbol in 1992.

features.role: Tryzub (small coat of arms of Ukraine) on a blue square inset
